title: S5: migrate worktree-parallelism — the one genuinely shapeless area, via a new narrative-sections scheme
action: This is the ONLY area of the eleven with no anchor-bearing sections: docs/specs/worktree-parallelism.md (225 lines) has 10 narrative '## ' headings (What problem this solves / The trust model / Registering a worktree / Entering / Returning / Routing rule / Cross-worktree holds / The three tiers / Boundary / Where it lives) and zero numbered B*/R*/E*/P* anchors. F9 forbids forcing it into ba-nine-section. INSTEAD, add a THIRD scheme to okf_migrate.mjs: `narrative-sections` — the anchors ARE the source's own '## ' headings, derived mechanically from the heading text (slugified, e.g. S-the-trust-model), never invented. This mirrors the existing flat-pattern-list precedent, which already treats '## [YYYYMMDD] title' headings as anchors. The anchor's TEXT for fidelity purposes is that section's body up to the next heading. Then run the normal loop: STEP 0 capture the pin (scheme narrative-sections, expected_counts with the heading count and unparsed_blocks) with the verbatim source copy hash-verified; STEP 1 author concepts under docs/knowledge/areas/worktree-parallelism/ splitting by topic, every heading-anchor owned by exactly one concept, terminology verbatim, canonical frontmatter; STEP 2 pointer stub with the full anchor map (D37) mapping each heading to its concept, reading-map row rewired; STEP 3 add --check worktree-parallelism to EXTRA_SUITES + MANDATORY_SUITE_ARGS; STEP 4 index regen, log.md entry, full chain (74 suites). RED-FIRST: add the scheme's tests (a heading becomes an anchor; a '###' subheading does NOT; a source with zero '## ' headings is refused rather than passed 0/0) and capture them red before implementing; then the usual one-anchor-unassigned gate red. IMPORTANT: the three shipped schemes must stay strict no-ops — re-verify all 9 existing pins after adding the scheme.

truths: red evidence: the narrative-sections scheme tests failed before implementation (verbatim), plus the one-anchor-unassigned gate red
STRICT NO-OP: all 9 pre-existing pins still derive their exact expected_counts after the scheme is added — --verify-pins output quoted
coverage: every '## ' heading of the pinned blob owned by exactly one concept, 0 duplicated, 0 lost
fidelity min/median quoted above the 0.60 floor with no concept edited
a source with zero '## ' headings is REFUSED under this scheme, never passed 0/0
knowledge check 0/0; index --check 0 stale; blob_sha verified
prohibitions: never invent numbered ids for a source that has none (D10) — the headings are the anchors
never force this area into ba-nine-section (F9)
never let the new scheme change any existing pin's derived counts
never edit a concept or lower a threshold to clear the floor

trace.outcome: worktree-parallelism migrated behind a D37 stub via a new narrative-sections scheme (headings ARE the anchors); 10 anchors / 7 concepts, fidelity 1.000, all 9 prior pins strict no-op, chain 74 suites green

    "coverage_gate_red": "FAIL okf_migrate --check worktree-parallelism: 10 anchors, 9 owned, 0 duplicated, 1 lost -- LOST in concepts: S-boundary-out-of-scope is claimed by no concept's bee.sources (expected one owner) (exit 1, S-boundary-out-of-scope claim removed from overview.md then restored)"
  },
  "strict_no_op_all_nine_prior_pins": "node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --verify-pins exit 0: PASS advisor-protocol 26 {4,9,6,7,0} | PASS doctrine-layer 39 {10,17,5,7,2} | PASS critical-patterns 47 {47,0} | PASS decision-memory 9 {0,9,0,0,0} | PASS verify-pipeline 14 {0,5,4,5,7} | PASS performance-log 23 {0,11,5,7,10} | PASS feedback-digest 29 {0,15,6,8,26} | PASS onboarding 58 {0,28,15,15,20} | PASS hook-runtime 81 {22,24,17,18,8} | PASS worktree-parallelism 10 {sections:10,total:10,unparsed_blocks:2}",
  "coverage": "PASS okf_migrate --check worktree-parallelism: 10 anchors, 10 owned, 0 duplicated, 0 lost — ground truth DERIVED from pinned blob df2f441bb88e9632a1f95d288331a3e356c68e19 (687ac590:docs/specs/worktree-parallelism.md, via git, scheme narrative-sections)",
  "fidelity": "fidelity floor 0.60 (F11): 10 anchors measured — min 1.000, median 1.000, max 1.000 (no concept edited to clear the floor)",
  "telemetry": "anchors_per_concept 1.43, concepts_per_100_source_lines 3.10 (10 anchors, 7 concepts, 226 source lines) — 1 pinned narrative-sections source of 10 pinned in total, fewer than 3 comparable samples, so telemetry REPORTS ONLY",
  "zero_heading_refusal": "extractByScheme('narrative-sections', <no ## heading>) throws code PIN_EMPTY_EXTRACTION; derivePin over a heading-less source with expected_counts.total 0 still exits 1 with PIN_EMPTY_EXTRACTION — never 0/0",
  "bundle": "bee knowledge check: 100 concept(s) in 115 file(s), 0 OKF error(s), 0 profile warning(s) — OK; knowledge index --check: 14 expected index file(s), 0 stale — OK",
  "suites": "node scripts/test_okf_pins.mjs PASS 371 assertions; node scripts/test_verify_manifest.mjs PASS (74 discovered, floor 59, 10 mandatory argument variants)",
  "chain": "node scripts/run_verify.mjs — PASS run_verify: 74 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=60931ms (exit 0)",
  "red_failure_evidence": "BEFORE (the prior behavior this change alters), captured verbatim: (1) the area was REFUSED by name — `node scripts/okf_migrate.mjs --verify-pins` printed \"SKIP-REFUSED worktree-parallelism: no anchor scheme declared — this area cannot be gated yet (F9/S5)\", and derivePinForArea(\"worktree-parallelism\") returned ok:false with code PIN_NO_SCHEME (asserted by test_okf_pins section 11 as it stood before this cell). (2) The scheme did not exist: test_okf_pins run against the pre-change okf_migrate.mjs failed with \"FAIL test_okf_pins: scripts/okf_migrate.mjs must export inventoryNarrativeSections (f2-11)\", \"must export slugifyHeading\", \"must export NARRATIVE_ANCHOR_PATTERN\", \"FAIL test_okf_pins: the `narrative-sections` scheme is registered in SCHEMES [\"ba-nine-section\",\"flat-pattern-list\"]\" and TypeError: M.inventoryNarrativeSections is not a function (exit 1) — full capture in .bee/tmp/f2-11/red-scheme.txt. (3) The coverage gate had no chain entry: run_verify discovered 73 suites and test_verify_manifest listed 9 mandatory argument variants, with SUITE_FLOOR_COUNT 58. (4) The area gate itself was red on the real coverage law once pinned but unauthored: \"FAIL okf_migrate --check worktree-parallelism: 10 anchors, 0 owned, 0 duplicated, 10 lost\" (concept directory missing; every anchor LOST in stub map and in concepts). AFTER: --verify-pins prints no SKIP-REFUSED line, --check worktree-parallelism exits 0 at 10/10 owned, and the chain carries 74 suites.",
  "deliberate_exceptions": "None. The F12 comparability key moved from pin.kind to pin.scheme; that is a deliberate behavior change to a shipped guard, proven a strict no-op on every pre-existing pin by test_okf_pins section 29 (the eight ba-nine-section areas regroup exactly as the eight kind:\"area\" pins did; critical-patterns stays alone exactly as kind:\"patterns\" did). The telemetry JSON key median_available_by_kind is renamed median_available_by_scheme accordingly — a diagnostic --telemetry output field, not consumed by any suite or chain entry (grepped)."
}
